lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਡੋਲਤ ਬੇੜੀ ਪਾਪ ਭਰੀ ਹੈ ਪਵਣੁ ਲਗੈ ਮਤੁ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ham ddolat berree paap bharee hai pavan lagai mat jaaee |
lines.english: My boat is wobbly and unsteady; it is filled with sins. The wind is rising - what if it tips over?
Raamkalee, First Mehl:
has the wisdom of the six Shaastras. ||4||5||
O Guru, my Saving Grace, please carry me across the world-ocean.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਨਮੁਖ ਸਿਧ ਭੇਟਣ ਕਉ ਆਏ ਨਿਹਚਉ ਦੇਹਿ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sanamukh sidh bhettan kau aae nihchau dehi vaddiaaee |1|
lines.english: As sunmukh, I have turned to the Guru; O my Perfect Master; please be sure to bless me with Your glorious greatness. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਹਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਅਵਿਨਾਸੀ ਹਉ ਤੁਝ ਕਉ ਬਲਿਹਾਰਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: dehi bhagat pooran avinaasee hau tujh kau balihaariaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Bless me with devotion to the perfect, imperishable Lord God; I am a sacrifice to You. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਧ ਸਾਧਿਕ ਜੋਗੀ ਅਰੁ ਜੰਗਮ ਏਕੁ ਸਿਧੁ ਜਿਨੀ ਧਿਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sidh saadhik jogee ar jangam ek sidh jinee dhiaaeaa |
lines.english: He alone is a Siddha, a seeker, a Yogi, a wandering pilgrim, who meditates on the One Perfect Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਸਤ ਪੈਰ ਸਿਝਤ ਤੇ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਅਖਰੁ ਜਿਨ ਕਉ ਆਇਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: parasat pair sijhat te suaamee akhar jin kau aaeaa |2|
lines.english: Touching the feet of the Lord Master, they are emancipated; they come to receive the Word of the Teachings. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰੁ ਪਰਮੇਸਰੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਭੇਟਿਓ ਸਾਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਨਿਬੇਰਾ ॥੩॥੬॥
lines.translit: gur paramesar naanak bhettio saachai sabad niberaa |3|6|
lines.english: Nanak has met the Guru, the Transcendent Lord God; through the True Word of His Shabad, he is set free. ||3||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪ ਤਪ ਸੰਜਮ ਕਰਮ ਨ ਜਾਨਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਜਪੀ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਤੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: jap tap sanjam karam na jaanaa naam japee prabh teraa |
lines.english: I know nothing of charity, meditation, self-discipline or religious rituals; I only chant Your Name, God.
Make your body a raft, to cross over.
Focus your consciousness in deep absorption on the Lord.
Raamkalee, First Mehl:
Day and night, that lamp shall burn unceasingly. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਦੀਵੈ ਸਭ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jit deevai sabh sojhee paae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: this lamp will bring total understanding. ||1||Pause||
Float such a lamp upon the water;
Deep within is the fire of desire; keep it in check.
So shape this lamp on the wheel of good actions.
This understanding is good clay;
a lamp made of such clay is acceptable to the Lord.
When He Himself grants His Grace,
In this world and in the next, this lamp shall be with you. ||2||
Within the heart, this lamp is permanently lit.
then, as Gurmukh, one may understand Him.
Wind does not shake it, or put it out.
It is not extinguished by water or wind.
Such a lamp will carry you across the water. ||3||
Its light reveals the Divine Throne.
The Kh'shaatriyas, Brahmins, Soodras and Vaishyas
cannot find its value, even by thousands of calculations.
O Nanak, he is emancipated. ||4||7||
If any of them lights such a lamp,
Raamkalee, First Mehl:
To place one's faith in Your Name, Lord, is true worship.
If a prayer is offered with truth and contentment,
the Lord will hear it, and call him in to sit by Him. ||1||
With an offering of Truth, one obtains a place to sit.
O Nanak, no one returns empty-handed;
The treasure I seek is the gift of Your Grace.
such is the Court of the True Lord. ||1||Pause||
Please bless this humble beggar - this is what I seek.
Please, pour Your Love into the cup of my heart.
This is Your pre-determined value. ||2||
The Sovereign Lord King becomes manifest to the Gurmukh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਕਿਛੁ ਕੀਆ ਸੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: jin kichh keea so kichh karai |
lines.english: The One who created everything, does everything.
He Himself appraises His own value.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕੋ ਆਵੈ ਨਾ ਕੋ ਜਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naa ko aavai naa ko jaae |3|
lines.english: He does not come, and He does not go. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕੁ ਧਿਕਾਰੁ ਕਹੈ ਮੰਗਤ ਜਨ ਮਾਗਤ ਮਾਨੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: lok dhikaar kahai mangat jan maagat maan na paaeaa |
lines.english: People curse at the beggar; by begging, he does not receive honor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਹ ਕੀਆ ਗਲਾ ਦਰ ਕੀਆ ਬਾਤਾ ਤੈ ਤਾ ਕਹਣੁ ਕਹਾਇਆ ॥੪॥੮॥
lines.translit: seh keea galaa dar keea baataa tai taa kehan kahaaeaa |4|8|
lines.english: O Lord, You inspire me to speak Your Words, and tell the Story of Your Court. ||4||8||
Raamkalee, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਗਰ ਮਹਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਬੂੰਦ ਮਹਿ ਸਾਗਰੁ ਕਵਣੁ ਬੁਝੈ ਬਿਧਿ ਜਾਣੈ ॥
lines.translit: saagar meh boond boond meh saagar kavan bujhai bidh jaanai |
lines.english: The drop is in the ocean, and the ocean is in the drop. Who understands, and knows this?

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਤਭੁਜ ਚਲਤ ਆਪਿ ਕਰਿ ਚੀਨੈ ਆਪੇ ਤਤੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: autabhuj chalat aap kar cheenai aape tat pachhaanai |1|
lines.english: He Himself creates the wondrous play of the world. He Himself contemplates it, and understands its true essence. ||1||
